key_points: New lottery system widely hated — seniority no longer counts
Lucille Clement consistently described as cramped, loud, and prison-like
Overflow housing sent students to hotels at semester start
key_quotes: God I fucking hate Noland — making our housing a gamble and giving majority to freshman is insane
Do not put Lucille Clement even on your list; it is awful
It's loud at all hours of the night, there's dirty laundry everywhere, the bathrooms can get disgusting

key_points: Loneliness is the #1 recurring complaint from freshmen and transfers
Clubs exist but many students struggle to find their community
Reddit used heavily to find friends — signals campus social scene is lacking
key_quotes: I have made essentially no friends while being on campus and it has made my college experience a little lonely
I haven't really made any friends or found any communities I click in
I am a freshman and I have 0 friends to be honest

key_points: Food quality dropped noticeably while meal plan prices went up
Limited selection and broken card reader went unfixed for a full semester
Multiple students say they stopped eating there and cook or eat out instead
key_quotes: The food this year seems to be worse than last year with a more limited selection
I just can't bring myself to eat there anymore, and have been making my own food or eating out
Mind you the meal plan prices went up this year

key_points: Counseling services exist and are mentioned positively by other students
Academic burnout and scholarship pressure compound mental health struggles
Campus political climate adds stress for LGBTQ+ and marginalized students
key_quotes: Insane academic burnout and high expectations my scholarships require of me don't make my experience any better
I don't even want my bachelors anymore. I don't even feel like I've retained any of the information
I can only call themselves trans online because they... severely damages everyone's experience at the school

key_points: One of the cheapest in-state tuitions in Tennessee — tied with TN Tech
GPA above 3.5 likely nets a $3k/year academic performance scholarship automatically
Some classes charge extra fees for coursework access ($112 for one course)
key_quotes: It is about half the cost of UTK
Not even my counselor knows what classes require you to pay for the coursework. It's $112 for 4 months
The main thing that has changed is Fees and cost of living — Arts and Sciences charges $50/credit hour in fees

key_points: Cyber security and CS programs well-regarded academically but job market locally is weak
Academic integrity question raised by Mike Israetel PhD scandal — ETSU never responded
Professors range from excellent to openly political MAGA — quality varies widely
key_quotes: The cyber security program here is decent. The job market in this area is not
I had an English professor who was full on MAGA and talked about it all the time
ETSU has yet to respond to the videos and articles discussing this [dissertation scandal]

key_points: President Noland repeatedly blamed by students for housing and DEI policy failures
Free speech officially protected but students report suppression of dissenting voices
Housing portal, email systems, and financial aid communication are consistently disorganized
key_quotes: ETSU has turned to shit under Nolan, it was depressing asf to watch
People come first, are treated with dignity and respect — lie
They'll cave to fascism because they have lots of alt and far right staff

key_points: Johnson City itself is LGBTQ-friendly; surrounding towns are not
Campus is walkable but accessibility for disabled students is described as 'abysmal'
Outdoor recreation and Appalachian scenery are genuine highlights nearby
key_quotes: Johnson City itself is very accepting of the LGBTQ+ community, don't count on the towns around it though
Ramps are horribly placed, the elevators are consistently broken — wheeling my wheelchair across the football field to get to class
It was pretty boring when I went there and it is definitely in the middle of nowhere

key_points: Local job market for CS and cyber security called 'abysmal' by multiple students
Graduates typically must relocate — Nashville minimum for tech jobs
Health sciences and social work programs have stronger local employment ties
key_quotes: I'm sure practically everyone in this comment section has already warned you, but the job market here in East Tenn for cyber security or any sort of comp sci is abysmal
The program is well known to be good for computer science as a whole. The job market cannot support all of the graduates locally
You would likely end up looking elsewhere for employment

key_points: Among cheapest in-state options in Tennessee — strong scholarship availability
Study abroad can actually cost less than a semester on campus
Rising fees, worse dining, and lottery housing erode perceived value
key_quotes: It saved me a lot of money so I don't regret it
My son is studying abroad in Japan this semester and it was actually cheaper than being on campus
The apartments are hella expensive and the price increase plus worse food doesn't add up

key_points: Conservative-leaning campus culture creates friction for LGBTQ+ and progressive students
Small campus feel with tight-knit programs, but social scene requires real effort
Students who embrace outdoor life and find their niche tend to thrive here
key_quotes: On campus it's less accepting, more so tolerating — it honestly seems hard
Anything is what you make it — there's much to do here if you look
I had an AMAZING time. Highly recommend staying in the smallest dorms like Lucy if you want community

key_points: **Administration:** President Noland blamed for DEI gutting, housing chaos, and policy failures
**Accessibility:** Wheelchair users report broken elevators, unusable ramps, and dangerous routes to class
**Political Climate:** TPUSA active on campus, roommate matching ignores LGBTQ+ safety preferences
key_quotes: They are overfilling housing and the overflow goes to hotels for weeks at the start of the semester
Disability services can be helpful in the classroom but not really anywhere else — etsu has a habit of misinforming people
They genuinely do not care [about LGBTQ+ roommate matching]. TPUSA and the forced-birthers are a plague that gets worse every year

key_points: Study abroad program offers cheaper semesters abroad with strong scholarships
Library Innovation Commons has 3D printers and laser engravers free to use
Outdoor Adventure program runs regular trips — underused and underadvertised
key_quotes: I studied abroad in Greece and it was actually cheaper to attend a semester there than ETSU — would highly recommend
On the second floor is the Innovation Commons with 3D printers, laser engraver, lots of stuff
There's an amazing outdoor adventure program that goes on trips a few times a semester
